checks.a_analysis: The conversion results are consistent with the source document's headings and text content. There are no invented sections or body text that cannot be traced back to the original PDF. While the table for the initial items is formatted differently in the JSON, the information content matches.
I verified the phone number (984-204-1487) and Conference ID (766 174 851#) provided in the meeting header. They match the source exactly.
Both pages are represented in the output. Sections corresponding to agenda items 1 through 13 are present, along with the meeting header and closing notes.

The agenda table (which includes the preliminary items and the first 6 numbered items) is captured in a JSON table element with 10 rows (1 header + 9 data rows). This matches the logical row count of the source table area.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion fails significantly on Item 5 (Consent Agenda) and Item 8 (Funding Committee Report). For Item 5, the entire sub-content for sub-items b, c, d, and e—which contains all the financial figures (Month-End Balance and Disbursement amounts) for the various funds—is missing. Additionally, sub-sub-items f.i and f.ii (Monthly Reports and Mitchell County request) are absent. For Item 8, the sub-item 'a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution' is entirely missing from the list.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5: April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707 ... i. Monthly Reports per PSAP ... Item 8: a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution (Roll Call Vote Required)..........Marsha Tapler
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 5 in JSON only contains the fund titles without figures or sub-sub-items. Item 8 in JSON only contains the top-level report title and presenters.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The textual data captured matches the wording of the source correctly, with the exception of the significant omissions noted in C5. Dates, names (e.g., Eric Boyette, Pokey Harris, Donna Wright), and legislation references (SL 2020-3) are accurate.

checks.a_analysis: No instances of duplicated text or sections were identified between the source and the output.
The heading hierarchy correctly captures the title 'AGENDA' as H1 and the meeting title as H2. No heading levels are skipped.

The main agenda table (reading_order 4) is structurally corrupted. While the header defines three columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter), the subsequent rows fail to maintain this structure. Most rows contain only one or two cells, incorrectly merging content from the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' columns into a single string. Additionally, the financial data associated with item 5 is omitted from the table structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table header lists 3 columns: Tab, Topic, Presenter. Row 1 (Item 1) should have '1.' in column 1, 'Chair's Opening Remarks' in column 2, and 'Eric Boyette' in column 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output table row for item 1 contains only two cells. Cell 2 is: 'Chair's Opening Remarks.............................................................................Eric Boyette'

checks.a_analysis: The reading order for the first page is logically flawed. The conversion places all top-level agenda items (1 through 6) into a single table at reading_order 4. It then lists the sub-items for item 5 (Consent Agenda) and item 6 (Executive Director Report) in separate lists at reading_order 5 and 6. This results in the main heading for item 6 appearing before the supporting details/sub-items of item 5, which contradicts the sequential flow of the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 (Consent Agenda) header is immediately followed by sub-items a) through f). Item 6 (Executive Director Report) follows after sub-item f) ii.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output table at reading_order 4 includes both the Item 5 and Item 6 headers. The detailed sub-items for Item 5 are not provided until reading_order 5.

checks.a_analysis: Multiple lists are missing data or nested items. In the Consent Agenda list (reading_order 5), the financial figures (Month-End Balance and Disbursement) for sub-items b, c, d, and e are entirely missing. The nested list items i and ii under item 5f are also absent. On page 2 (reading_order 11), sub-item 8a ('FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution') is missing from the list.
checks.b_evidence_source: b) NG 911 Fund/Account [followed by financial data] ... f) Grant Project Updates per Reports i. Monthly Reports per PSAP ... 8. Funding Committee Report a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution
checks.b_evidence_output: The output list at reading_order 5 for item 5b contains only 'b) NG 911 Fund/Account'. Item 8 in reading_order 11 lacks its sub-item 'a'.

checks.a_analysis: No links were injected into the document. Phone numbers and conference IDs are present as text but not converted to links, which is acceptable.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly compromised. In the source PDF, the agenda follows a clear hierarchical structure where sub-items (a, b, c, etc.) are nested directly under their parent items (5 and 6). In the HTML output, these sub-items are detached from their parent rows and rendered as separate bulleted lists, which breaks the logical and visual flow of the meeting agenda. Furthermore, substantial portions of the content are missing: the financial data (Month-End Balances and Disbursements) under item 5 is completely absent in the HTML. Additionally, items 7-13 are rendered in a different format (plain text list with leader dots) compared to items 1-6 (table), creating an inconsistent visual layout.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 (Consent Agenda) contains nested sub-items a-f, including indented financial data like 'April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707'. Item 6 similarly contains nested sub-items a-g.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML displays Item 5 and 6 as table rows, but their sub-items are extracted into standalone bulleted lists below the table. All financial data (dollar amounts and descriptions) is missing from the list under item 5.
checks.b_evidence_location: Items 5 and 6, middle of page 1 / top half of HTML rendering.

checks.a_analysis: The table structure used for the main agenda is visually broken due to column misalignment. Although the HTML includes 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' headers, the data is not correctly placed within these columns. For the first three unnumbered rows, the topic text and presenter names are both merged into the 'Tab' column. For the numbered rows (1-6), the topic and presenter names are merged into the 'Topic' column. Consequently, the 'Presenter' column is entirely empty for all rows in the rendered table, failing to provide the structured information present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF shows '1.' in the Tab column, 'Chair’s Opening Remarks...' in the Topic column, and 'Eric Boyette' in the Presenter column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table for the same row shows '1.' in the Tab column, but 'Chair's Opening Remarks............................................................Eric Boyette' combined in the Topic column, leaving the Presenter column empty.

checks.a_analysis: The primary logo (NC DIT 911 Board) is present in the rendered HTML. It appears as a large central image and again as a smaller image at the bottom, corresponding to its presence on pages 1 and 2 of the source PDF.

There is no visual duplication of body content paragraphs or tables in the HTML output. The repetition of the logo at the bottom is consistent with its appearance on the second page of the source document.
